text.p: The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Keep clear of fallen power lines;
* Beware of fallen trees or debris on the road.
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.

warning-info.text: No further warnings will be issued for this event.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Heavy rainfall no longer expected across the North East Pastoral.

text.p: Weather Situation: The upper trough that has been affecting eastern parts of SA is clearing to the east. This combined with a drier and more stable airmass gradually moving into the area has led to an end of the heavy rainfall risk.
Flood Watches and Warnings remain current for the area as a result of the recent heavy rainfall. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
Flood Watches and Warnings remain current for the area as a result of the recent heavy rainfall. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
